segments.text: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron,
Say to the Israelites:
Of all the animals that live on land, these are the ones you may eat:
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: You may eat any animal that has a divided hoof and that chews the cud.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: There are some that only chew the cud or only have a divided hoof, but you must not eat them. The camel, though it chews the cud, does not have a divided hoof; it is ceremonially unclean for you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The hyrax, though it chews the cud, does not have a divided hoof; it is unclean for you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The rabbit, though it chews the cud, does not have a divided hoof; it is unclean for you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And the pig, though it has a divided hoof, does not chew the cud; it is unclean for you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: You must not eat their meat or touch their carcasses; they are unclean for you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Of all the creatures living in the water of the seas and the streams you may eat any that have fins and scales.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But all creatures in the seas or streams that do not have fins and scales—whether among all the swarming things or among all the other living creatures in the water—you are to regard as unclean.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And since you are to regard them as unclean, you must not eat their meat; you must regard their carcasses as unclean.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Anything living in the water that does not have fins and scales is to be regarded as unclean by you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: These are the birds you are to regard as unclean and not eat because they are unclean: the eagle, the vulture, the black vulture,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: the red kite, any kind of black kite,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: any kind of raven,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: the horned owl, the screech owl, the gull, any kind of hawk,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: the little owl, the cormorant, the great owl,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: the white owl, the desert owl, the osprey,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: the stork, any kind of heron, the hoopoe and the bat.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: All flying insects that walk on all fours are to be regarded as unclean by you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: There are, however, some flying insects that walk on all fours that you may eat: those that have jointed legs for hopping on the ground.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Of these you may eat any kind of locust, katydid, cricket or grasshopper.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But all other flying insects that have four legs you are to regard as unclean.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: You will make yourselves unclean by these; whoever touches their carcasses will be unclean till evening.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Whoever picks up one of their carcasses must wash their clothes, and they will be unclean till evening.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Every animal that does not have a divided hoof or that does not chew the cud is unclean for you; whoever touches the carcass of any of them will be unclean.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Of all the animals that walk on all fours, those that walk on their paws are unclean for you; whoever touches their carcasses will be unclean till evening.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Anyone who picks up their carcasses must wash their clothes, and they will be unclean till evening. These animals are unclean for you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Of the animals that move along the ground, these are unclean for you: the weasel, the rat, any kind of great lizard,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: the gecko, the monitor lizard, the wall lizard, the skink and the chameleon.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Of all those that move along the ground, these are unclean for you. Whoever touches them when they are dead will be unclean till evening.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: When one of them dies and falls on something, that article, whatever its use, will be unclean, whether it is made of wood, cloth, hide or sackcloth. Put it in water; it will be unclean till evening, and then it will be clean.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If one of them falls into a clay pot, everything in it will be unclean, and you must break the pot.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Any food you are allowed to eat that has come into contact with water from any such pot is unclean, and any liquid that is drunk from such a pot is unclean.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Anything that one of their carcasses falls on becomes unclean; an oven or cooking pot must be broken up. They are unclean, and you are to regard them as unclean.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: A spring, however, or a cistern for collecting water remains clean, but anyone who touches one of these carcasses is unclean.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If a carcass falls on any seeds that are to be planted, they remain clean.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But if water has been put on the seed and a carcass falls on it, it is unclean for you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If an animal that you are allowed to eat dies, anyone who touches its carcass will be unclean till evening.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Anyone who eats some of its carcass must wash their clothes, and they will be unclean till evening. Anyone who picks up the carcass must wash their clothes, and they will be unclean till evening.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Every creature that moves along the ground is to be regarded as unclean; it is not to be eaten.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: You are not to eat any creature that moves along the ground, whether it moves on its belly or walks on all fours or on many feet; it is unclean.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Do not defile yourselves by any of these creatures. Do not make yourselves unclean by means of them or be made unclean by them.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I am the Lord your God; consecrate yourselves and be holy, because I am holy. Do not make yourselves unclean by any creature that moves along the ground.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I am the Lord, who brought you up out of Egypt to be your God; therefore be holy, because I am holy.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: These are the regulations concerning animals, birds, every living thing that moves about in the water and every creature that moves along the ground.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: You must distinguish between the unclean and the clean, between living creatures that may be eaten and those that may not be eaten.
segments.speaker: The Lord
